summary: Replayed ADR 0339 from the latest realistic `origin/main` tip by keeping the governed reference deployment sample catalog, replaceable provider profiles, and render-and-validate bootstrap tooling on the exact-main `0.178.4` release tree, then refreshing canonical truth and release surfaces from that release state.

verification.observed: The rebased ADR 0339 branch proof passed sample validation, targeted tests, rendered-inventory loading, and repo-gate checks on `origin/main@c48bdae01f6fe4d0df004e247baa8a3d9f6e013b`, preserved in `receipts/live-applies/evidence/2026-04-04-ws-0339-reference-samples-validate-r2-0.178.3.txt`, `receipts/live-applies/evidence/2026-04-04-ws-0339-targeted-tests-r2-0.178.3.txt`, `receipts/live-applies/evidence/2026-04-04-ws-0339-render-r2-0.178.3.txt`, `receipts/live-applies/evidence/2026-04-04-ws-0339-rendered-inventory-r2-0.178.3.json`, `receipts/live-applies/evidence/2026-04-04-ws-0339-workstream-surfaces-r2-0.178.3.txt`, and `receipts/live-applies/evidence/2026-04-04-ws-0339-validate-repo-r4-0.178.3.txt`.

Before the exact-main release cut, `LV3_SKIP_OUTLINE_SYNC=1 python3 scripts/release_manager.py status --json` still reported repo version `0.178.3`, platform version `0.130.98`, and only the pre-existing waiver blockers through `2026-04-06`, while `--dry-run --bump patch` still resolved the next numbered release to `0.178.4`, preserved in `receipts/live-applies/evidence/2026-04-04-ws-0339-release-status-r2-0.178.3.json` and `receipts/live-applies/evidence/2026-04-04-ws-0339-release-dry-run-r2-0.178.3.txt`.

The lower-level exact-main release replay successfully wrote repository release `0.178.4`, archived the eight pending live-applied workstreams, refreshed canonical truth, and regenerated the protected release surfaces, preserved in `receipts/live-applies/evidence/2026-04-04-ws-0339-mainline-release-lower-level-r2-0.178.4.txt`.

The finished exact-main `0.178.4` tree passed `./scripts/validate_repo.sh agent-standards workstream-surfaces data-models generated-docs generated-portals`, `make validate`, `make remote-validate`, and `make pre-push-gate`. The shared remote runner still hit the familiar remote `atlas-lint` connectivity timeout, but both `make remote-validate` and `make pre-push-gate` reran only the unresolved `atlas-lint` check locally and merged that passing fallback into the recorded gate status, preserved in `receipts/live-applies/evidence/2026-04-04-ws-0339-mainline-validate-repo-r2-0.178.4.txt`, `receipts/live-applies/evidence/2026-04-04-ws-0339-mainline-make-validate-r1-0.178.4.txt`, `receipts/live-applies/evidence/2026-04-04-ws-0339-mainline-remote-validate-r1-0.178.4.txt`, and `receipts/live-applies/evidence/2026-04-04-ws-0339-mainline-pre-push-gate-r1-0.178.4.txt`.

notes: This receipt becomes the canonical exact-main audit trail for ADR 0339 while preserving the earlier branch-local receipt as the first proof that the reference deployment sample flow worked on the rebased latest-main tree.
Repository release `0.178.4` is the first numbered repo version to include ADR 0339.

ADR 0339 changes fork bootstrap assets, onboarding packs, and repository validation behavior rather than a running service on the current deployment, so the platform version context remains `0.130.98`.

The final exact-main validation bundle succeeded end to end; only the already-known remote-runner `atlas-lint` connectivity issue required the repo's built-in unresolved-check local fallback before the merged gate status could be marked passing.
